sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Nio launches aftermarket assisted driving indicator light upgrade for Chinese customers
feed.summary: Nio has introduced an aftermarket assisted driving indicator light upgrade service for Chinese customers, effective April 27, 2026, to enhance the experience of existing vehicles. This upgrade, costing 998 yuan ($146) with a 10 yuan deposit discount, features a dual-color light design for turn signals and a blue light for seven assisted driving scenarios. It is compatible with several older Nio models, bringing a feature that is becoming standard on newly launched EVs.

feed.title: Sealed Air Corp (LTS:0L4F) Stock Earnings Transcripts
feed.summary: This article provides access to the earnings transcripts for Sealed Air Corp (LTS:0L4F), noting that the stock has been delisted. It includes key financial metrics such as market cap, enterprise value, PE ratio, and PB ratio. Access to the full content requires a professional membership or a 7-day free trial.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Shares Details About When He Bought Wells Fargo’s (WFC) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed his earlier purchase of Wells Fargo (WFC) stock at $33, praising CEO Charlie Scharf. The article highlights recent analyst ratings for WFC, with KGI Securities downgrading it to Hold with an $88 target, and Jefferies initiating coverage with a Buy rating and a $100 target after the Federal Reserve lifted the bank's asset cap. Despite WFC's performance, the author suggests higher potential returns from specific AI stocks.

feed.title: Biogen is entitled to damages in Tecfidera case rules Regional Court Hamburg
feed.summary: The Hamburg Regional Court has ruled that Biogen is entitled to damages from several generics companies, including Viatris, Hexal, Neuraxpharm, and Zentiva, for selling their dimethyl fumarate generics before the market exclusivity for Tecfidera ended. Biogen asserts that its market exclusivity ran until February 2024, claiming hundreds of millions in damages due to lost sales. The court's decision establishes Biogen's entitlement to damages, with the specific amount yet to be determined, noting the generics companies acted at least negligently despite legal uncertainties.

feed.title: Avery Dennison Invests $75M in Wiliot to Scale Physical AI
feed.summary: Avery Dennison has made a $75 million minority investment in Wiliot, a leader in Physical AI for supply chains. This investment deepens their existing partnership, making Avery Dennison Wiliot's preferred partner for inlay design, manufacturing, and commercial efforts. The collaboration aims to accelerate the adoption of digital identities on physical items, expanding sensor technologies and solutions for improved visibility and condition monitoring across various industries.

feed.title: Autodesk nominates Omar Abbosh to board of directors
feed.summary: Autodesk has nominated Omar Abbosh, currently CEO of Pearson and a former Microsoft and Accenture executive, to its board of directors. This nomination follows Stephen Milligan's decision not to seek re-election, ensuring the board maintains 11 members with a majority of independent directors. Abbosh brings significant experience in technology, strategy, and business transformation to Autodesk's board.

feed.title: Pizza Hut is closing hundreds of restaurants: See an updated list of doomed locations for 2026
feed.summary: Yum Brands is proceeding with its plan to close hundreds of underperforming Pizza Hut locations, with a Fast Company analysis revealing numerous closures across the U.S. in recent months. CFO Ranjith Roy stated these closures, part of the "Hut Forward" turnaround strategy, are expected to be completed by July 1, 2026. Despite domestic closures, Pizza Hut continues to expand globally, having opened over a thousand new locations in 65 countries last year.

feed.title: International Business Machines (IBM) is One of Jim Cramer’s Top Quantum Computing Stocks
feed.summary: Jim Cramer recommends International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) as a top quantum computing stock, having consistently backed the company throughout 2025. He distinguishes IBM's quantum computing efforts as having "actual quantum" technology, unlike other companies he deems "science projects." Despite a recent share price target reduction by Jefferies, the firm maintains a Buy rating, anticipating IBM could surpass first-quarter analyst estimates.

feed.title: Google stock hits 52-week high at $349.08 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Google's stock achieved a new 52-week high of $349.08, with shares currently trading at $349.62 and a market capitalization of $4.21 trillion, reflecting a 113% surge over the past year for Alphabet Inc Class A. This stellar performance is driven by investor confidence in the tech giant's innovation in cloud computing and AI, strong financial health with more cash than debt, and a "GREAT" financial health score, according to InvestingPro. The company also faces an expanded antitrust investigation in Brazil but continues its technological advancements through partnerships like Google DeepMind's collaboration with South Korea on the K-Moonshot project.

feed.title: Georgia Power files RFP for up to 6,000MW of new dispatchable energy resources
feed.summary: Georgia Power has filed an RFP for 2,000MW – 6,000MW of new dispatchable capacity resources, including thermal generation, energy storage, and battery storage-plus-renewables, to meet projected needs for 2032-2033. The utility is also seeking approval for an additional 385MW of supplemental solar resources through its CARES program. This proposal follows previous approvals for 9,900MW of new generation, reinforcing the state's interest in advancing BESS projects and meeting stringent criteria for large-load customers.

feed.title: Union Pacific Honors 138 Chemical Safety Winners
feed.summary: Union Pacific Railroad has recognized 138 companies with its 2025 Pinnacle Award for their exceptional commitment to safely transporting hazardous materials by rail. These companies demonstrated strong release prevention protocols and achieved zero non-accident releases of regulated hazardous materials shipments. Union Pacific emphasizes shared responsibility in safety, aiming to protect communities and ensure the safe movement of the rail network.

feed.title: KKR, Capital Group Launch Asia Hybrid Credit Fund During Private Credit Turbulence
feed.summary: KKR & Co. and Capital Group are partnering to launch a public-private credit fund in Asia, aiming to provide a more liquid, cheaper, and transparent option for wealth management in private markets. This initiative comes amidst a turbulent fundraising environment, with the fund targeting both public and private investments. The move follows a previous successful collaboration and significant fundraising efforts by KKR and other firms in the Asia-Pacific private credit space, despite stiff competition and investor selectivity.

feed.title: International Business Machines (IBM): One of the Best Low Risk High Growth Stocks?
feed.summary: International Business Machines (IBM) is transforming into a high-margin software company, driven by its watsonx AI platform and Red Hat acquisition, rather than primarily a hardware vendor. The company’s focus on providing secure AI solutions for regulated industries and a recent surge in mainframe sales are contributing to strong recurring revenue and projected free cash flow of $15.7 billion for 2026, marking its highest in over a decade. While IBM presents significant potential as a low-risk, high-growth stock, the article also suggests exploring other AI stocks with potentially higher and faster returns.

feed.title: Boeing, U.S. Navy Achieve Successful MQ-25A Test Flight
feed.summary: Boeing and the U.S. Navy have successfully completed the first test flight of an operational MQ-25A Stingray, marking a significant step towards carrier integration and naval aviation. The unmanned aircraft demonstrated autonomous taxi, take-off, flight, landing, and control from the ground station during a two-hour flight. This milestone advances the program closer to safely integrating the Stingray into the carrier air wing, providing extended range and freeing F/A-18s for their primary combat roles.

feed.title: NVR (NVR) director Michael DeVito adds 11 NVR shares in open-market buy
feed.summary: NVR, Inc. director Michael J. DeVito purchased 11 shares of company common stock in an open-market transaction on April 24, 2026, at a price of $6,699.50 per share. This purchase increases his direct ownership to 25 shares. The filing indicates a positive sentiment and signals confidence from the insider.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) Price Target Raised to $47.00 at TD Cowen
feed.summary: TD Cowen increased its price target for Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) to $47.00 from $46.00, maintaining a "buy" rating, which suggests an 18.9% potential upside. Despite this, analyst sentiment on Southwest is mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $44.36, as some firms raise targets while others lower them. The airline reported Q1 2026 EPS of $0.45, meeting expectations, but missed revenue forecasts, reporting $7.25 billion against an expected $8.46 billion, and provided Q2 2026 EPS guidance between $0.35 and $0.65.

feed.title: Wedbush Raises Price Target on Seagate Technology Holdings to $700 From $430, Keeps Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Wedbush has increased its price target for Seagate Technology Holdings (STX) to $700 from $430, while maintaining an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook from the analyst firm regarding the data storage systems manufacturer. The article also lists recent analyst activities and company news related to Seagate.

feed.title: Micron Technology Inc Stock (MU) Opened Up by 3.32% on Apr 27: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) stock surged 3.32% on strong demand for AI memory, exceeding Q2 2026 financial expectations and providing optimistic guidance. The company's High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) capacity is fully committed for 2026, indicating robust order visibility and pricing power in the AI-driven memory market. Analysts maintain "Buy" ratings and increased price targets, reflecting confidence in Micron's outlook.

feed.title: Honeywell Stock Falls After Q1 2026 Earnings Miss: What the June 29 Aerospace Spin Means for Investors
feed.summary: Honeywell reported a mixed Q1 2026, with revenue missing estimates but EPS beating them, leading to a stock dip. The company also announced a confirmed June 29 spin-off date for its Aerospace segment, along with several business sales and a Quantinuum IPO filing. Despite near-term headwinds from the Middle East conflict and supply chain issues, particularly in Aerospace, the company maintains a positive outlook for the second half of 2026 and 2027, driven by strong backlog and segment-specific growth.

feed.title: Xcel Energy Locks In Google Deal: Can XEL Break $100 in 2026?
feed.summary: Xcel Energy (XEL) has secured a significant deal to power Google's new data center in Minnesota, contributing to a 24% rise in its stock from its 52-week low. This agreement highlights Xcel's pivot from a traditional utility to an AI infrastructure provider, supported by a $60 billion capital plan and a target of 6 gigawatts in contracted data center capacity by 2027. Despite potential wildfire liabilities, analysts and TIKR's model suggest significant upside for XEL stock, with projections far exceeding its current price.

feed.title: Hackers impersonate Microsoft Teams help desk to breach corporate networks
feed.summary: A new report from Mandiant reveals that a threat cluster, UNC6692, is impersonating Microsoft Teams help desk workers to distribute data-stealing malware. The attack involves email flooding, phishing through Teams, and tricking victims into installing a malicious browser extension called SnowBelt. This extension acts as a backdoor, allowing attackers to access and navigate corporate systems, utilizing social engineering tactics like browser-specific prompts and deliberate login failures to enhance their success.
feed.source: The Record from Recorded Future News
feed.source_domain: The Record from Recorded Future News

feed.title: Rising AI Fears Hurt CoStar Group (CSGP) in Q1
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) experienced a stock decline in Q1 2026, primarily attributed to market fears regarding the impact of AI on various industries, including software and information services. Despite this, Baron Focused Growth Fund continues to hold CSGP, citing its strong data assets, growth opportunities in real estate information, and a robust business model with high recurring revenue. The fund believes the market is overreacting to AI risks without fundamental evidence and expects CoStar's cash flow to improve over time as investment in Homes.com moderates.

feed.title: Domino's profits fall as consumers curb spending based on rising costs
feed.summary: Domino's Pizza Inc. reported a decline in first-quarter net income by 6.6% to $139.8 million, primarily due to investment losses, despite an increase in total revenues to $1.15 billion. The company attributes the profit slump to an unfavorable swing in the valuation of its investment in DPC Dash Ltd in China, alongside consumers curbing spending due to rising costs and a weak labor market. Despite this, Domino's is driving sales through promotional offers and continues its global expansion, adding 180 new stores, and has authorized a new $1 billion share repurchase program.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) Price Target Raised to $145.00 at Raymond James Financial
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has increased its price target for ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) to $145.00 from $118.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating, indicating a potential 17.88% upside. This comes despite ConocoPhillips missing recent quarterly earnings estimates and significant insider share sales. The energy producer currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, with an average price target of $132.44.

feed.title: The Real Brokerage Acquires RE/MAX Holdings for $880 Million
feed.summary: The Real Brokerage Inc. announced its acquisition of RE/MAX Holdings Inc. for approximately $880 million. The combined entity, to be known as Real REMAX Group, will encompass over 100,000 real estate professionals and will be headquartered in Miami with significant operations in Denver. The transaction, expected to close in the second half of the year, aims to create a more innovative and connected real estate ecosystem.

feed.title: Fraud EDU: Fake surveys; fake summons – how Regions Bank is helping consumers fight back
feed.summary: Regions Bank is actively educating consumers about prevalent digital scams, specifically focusing on fake court summons and phony online surveys. These schemes aim to steal personal and financial information by exploiting trust and fear. The bank emphasizes vigilance and provides resources to help customers recognize red flags and protect themselves from evolving fraud tactics.

feed.title: AIG Completes CEO Succession Plan With Anderson to Takes Reins
feed.summary: American International Group Inc. (AIG) officially announced that Eric Anderson will become its new president and CEO on June 1, succeeding Peter Zaffino who will transition to executive chair of the board. This announcement on April 27, 2026, marks the successful conclusion of a succession plan first revealed in January. Anderson, formerly of Aon Plc, joined AIG in February, while Zaffino has led the company since March 2021.

feed.title: Why Intuitive Surgical Is a Strong Buy as Robotic Surgery Adoption Accelerates
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) is considered a strong buy despite a recent stock decline, trading at a 20% discount to its 52-week high. The company reported robust Q1 2026 results with non-GAAP EPS of $2.50 and revenue up 23% year-over-year, driven by accelerated adoption of its da Vinci 5 systems. Analysts at 24/7 Wall St. set a price target of $615.93, indicating a 28% upside potential as robotic surgery adoption continues to grow.

feed.title: Hershey’s new sustainability strategy offers blueprint for balancing business & ESG needs
feed.summary: Hershey has unveiled a new sustainability strategy called "Source. Make. Delight." which shifts from long-term pledges to practical, data-driven execution across sourcing, manufacturing, and consumer-facing innovation. This approach aims to embed sustainability into core business operations, addressing the tensions between cost, resilience, and impact while navigating evolving consumer expectations and regulatory environments. The strategy is designed to be an evolving internal operating system, providing a blueprint for other companies to integrate ESG into their day-to-day decision-making.

feed.title: KeyBanc raises Reliance Steel stock price target on strong results
feed.summary: KeyBanc increased its price target for Reliance Steel & Aluminum (NYSE:RS) to $378 from $341, maintaining an Overweight rating, following the company's strong first-quarter 2026 results. The analyst adjusted projections based on improved gross margins and new contracts. Reliance Steel is viewed as a core industrial holding, supported by strong free cash flow, diversified macro exposure, and consistent dividend increases.

feed.title: Assessing Williams-Sonoma (WSM) Valuation After Recent Share Price Volatility
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma (WSM) has experienced recent share price volatility, with a 1-day decline of 1% and a 7-day decline of 6%, though its 1-year and 3-year total shareholder returns are significantly positive. The stock is currently trading at $190.55, which is about a 20% discount to one intrinsic value estimate of $198.74, suggesting it might be undervalued based on growth and margin assumptions. However, another valuation angle using the P/E ratio suggests the stock is richly tagged compared to its fair ratio and peer average, trading at 20.8x P/E against a fair ratio of 16.2x.

feed.title: MiniMed not aware of compromises, doesn’t expect impact from Medtronic cyber breach
feed.summary: MiniMed has stated in an SEC filing that it does not expect to be materially impacted by a recent cybersecurity breach at its parent company, Medtronic. Medtronic confirmed an unauthorized party accessed data in some of its corporate IT systems but noted no impact on products, patient safety, or financial reporting. MiniMed emphasized that its own IT systems remain uncompromised and it anticipates no material effect on its business or financial results.

feed.title: Auto-Callable Intuit-Linked Notes (JPM) — 17.75%+ Contingent Rate
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes linked to Intuit Inc. common stock, with an expected pricing on April 30, 2026, and settlement on May 5, 2026. These notes offer a minimum contingent interest rate of 17.75% per annum, payable if Intuit's closing price meets an interest barrier of 50% of its initial value on review dates. The notes are callable if the stock price is at or above its initial value on certain review dates; however, investors face principal loss if the notes are not called and the final value falls below the trigger value.

feed.title: Corning stock gets expensive and overbought: will itt crash after earnings?
feed.summary: Corning stock has surged over 300% this year, fueled by its partnership with Meta Platforms and AI hype, but concerns are rising about its valuation ahead of its earnings release. Its forward P/E ratio of 56 is significantly higher than the sector median, and technical analysis suggests the stock is overbought and may experience a pullback, with a potential target of $120. Analysts acknowledge strong future outlooks but caution about the current pricing leaving little room for error.

feed.title: Citic Adjusts Price Target on Texas Instruments to $310 From $240, Maintains Add Rating
feed.summary: Citic has increased its price target for Texas Instruments (TXN) to $310 from $240, while reaffirming an "Add" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook by the analyst firm on the semiconductor company's prospects. The article also provides a brief overview of Texas Instruments' business segments and geographical sales distribution.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley reiterates Equalweight on Domino’s Pizza stock at $430
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reiterated an Equalweight rating on Domino's Pizza (NYSE:DPZ) with a $430 price target, despite the company's recent results falling below expectations and ongoing stock pressure. The firm noted that the 3% U.S. same-store sales target appears out of reach, and company store margins remain pressured, leading to a modest earnings miss. While share repurchases totaled $170 million year-to-date, Morgan Stanley believes that a historically cheap valuation alone is not a sufficient catalyst for the stock, currently trading near its 52-week low.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts American Electric Power Price Target to $136 From $137, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has adjusted its price target for American Electric Power (AEP) to $136, down from $137, while maintaining an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst revisions for AEP, with some raising and others lowering their price targets for the utility company. American Electric Power is a major electricity producer and distributor, with significant operations in generation, transmission, and distribution.

feed.title: Algorhythm Holdings, Inc. to Present at Market Movers Investor Summit on May 5, 2026
feed.summary: Algorhythm Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: RIME) will participate in the Market Movers Investor Summit on May 5, 2026, with CEO Gary Atkinson presenting at 1:40 PM ET. The summit, held at the historic Bank of New York, will feature presentations, one-on-one meetings, and discussions with notable figures like Alex Rodriguez and Grant Cardone. Algorhythm, through its SemiCab platform, specializes in AI-powered logistics solutions to optimize global supply chains.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts DTE Energy Price Target to $155 From $156, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has adjusted its price target for DTE Energy (DTE) to $155, down from $156, while maintaining an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This update reflects a slight modification in the analyst's valuation of the electric utility company. DTE Energy specializes in electricity production and distribution, serving millions of customers in Michigan, along with energetic product trading and natural gas transmission.

feed.title: Halliburton wraps up Greenland frontier exploration deal
feed.summary: Halliburton has completed a frontier exploration deal in Greenland, with Greenland Energy targeting a two-well onshore drilling campaign this year. The deal focuses on exploration activities in the region, as reported by Upstream.

feed.title: Algorhythm CEO Gary Atkinson takes May 5 Wall Street summit slot
feed.summary: Algorhythm Holdings (NASDAQ: RIME) CEO Gary Atkinson will present at the Market Movers Investor Summit on May 5, 2026, at 1:40 PM ET on Wall Street, an event featuring company presentations, fireside chats, and one-on-one meetings. This participation highlights the company's continuous efforts in investor outreach following recent milestones like strong 2025 revenue growth and the rising Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) of its SemiCab platform. The announcement emphasizes Algorhythm's AI-powered logistics strategy and its commitment to increasing visibility within the investment community.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on Principal Financial Group to $100 From $87, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for Principal Financial Group (PFG) to $100, up from the previous $87, while maintaining an "Equalweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions on PFG, with several firms

feed.title: Relay Therapeutics (Nasdaq: RLAY) plans Phase 3 breast cancer trial for zovegalisib
feed.summary: Relay Therapeutics announced plans for a Phase 3 frontline trial of its PI3Kα inhibitor, zovegalisib, in combination with atirmociclib and an aromatase inhibitor for PIK3CA-mutated, HR+/HER2- advanced or metastatic breast cancer. This decision follows promising early clinical data from a Phase 1/2 trial, which showed a 44% objective response rate and manageable safety in heavily pre-treated patients. Pfizer will supply the other drugs for the trial, which is expected to begin in early 2027.

feed.title: Callable contingent-interest notes from JPMorgan Chase (JPM) with 9.50%+ coupon
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Callable Contingent Interest Notes due March 30, 2028, with a minimum contingent interest rate of 9.50% per annum. Payments are tied to the performance of the Nasdaq-100® Technology Sector, Russell 2000®, and S&P 500® indices. The notes can be called early by the issuer, and investors face principal loss if the least performing index falls below its Trigger Value of 55.00% at maturity.

feed.title: Related Digital and Blackstone secure $16 billion to build Oracle's Michigan AI data center
feed.summary: Related Digital and Blackstone have successfully secured $16 billion to finance the construction of Oracle's new AI data center in Michigan. This significant investment will support the development of a critical infrastructure project for Oracle's artificial intelligence capabilities.

feed.title: Callable Treasury‑linked notes from JPMorgan (NYSE: JPM) with ≥6.60% factor
feed.summary: JPMorgan Financial is offering callable senior notes linked to the 10-Year Constant Maturity Treasury Rate, featuring a variable interest rate of at least 6.60% per annum. Interest accrues only on days when the Reference Rate is at or below a 5.00% barrier, with quarterly payments beginning July 28, 2026, and a maturity date of April 28, 2031. The notes are callable by JPMorgan on specified quarterly Redemption Dates, and investors face risks including credit risk and the variable nature of interest payments.

feed.title: SNDL, Inc. Investigated by the Portnoy Law Firm
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has launched an investigation into potential securities fraud involving SNDL, Inc. on behalf of investors. The firm's inquiry follows a significant 13.12% drop in SNDL's stock price on December 15, 2025, which occurred after the company announced a structural amendment to its cannabis retail expansion strategy, particularly concerning its acquisition agreement with 1CM Inc. The investigation focuses on the impact of this change, which involved splitting the acquisition into two closings due to regulatory approval timelines, leading to a loss of investor confidence.

feed.title: Sirius XM: iHeartMedia Deal Could Put The Cash Flow Thesis At Risk (NASDAQ:SIRI)
feed.summary: Sirius XM's investment case, heavily reliant on predictable free cash flow and shareholder returns, faces significant risk from a potential iHeartMedia deal. While the acquisition could add incremental cash flow and some growth opportunities, it also introduces high leverage and limited organic growth for Sirius XM. The author views the M&A as potentially more negative than beneficial, advising caution until greater clarity emerges.

feed.title: Superior Group of Companies (SGC) Projected to Post Earnings on Monday
feed.summary: Superior Group of Companies (NASDAQ:SGC) is expected to announce its Q1 2026 earnings after market close on Monday, May 4th, with analysts forecasting $0.02 EPS and $138.21 million in revenue. The company recorded higher-than-expected earnings of $0.23 EPS in the previous quarter against a consensus of $0.20. Despite a high dividend yield of 4.8%, the payout ratio exceeding 121% raises concerns about its sustainability.

feed.title: Datavault AI Announces Execution of Binding Term Sheet for $120 Million Cash Contribution From Scilex Holding Company to Fund 100-City GPU Expansion of Quantum-Ready SanQtum Platform
feed.summary: Datavault AI (NASDAQ: DVLT) has executed a binding term sheet with Scilex Holding Company (NASDAQ: SCLX) for a $120 million cash contribution. This funding will facilitate the expansion of Datavault AI’s quantum-ready GPU infrastructure across an estimated 100 cities in the United States, forming a "Quantum-Ready Edge Network" with an aggregate annual revenue potential of $10 billion to $100 billion. Scilex will receive a portion of the revenues generated by this network, with payment tiers set at 30%, 15%, and 5% as certain cumulative caps are met.
